title: Blind media-meer — SFrame-over-MLS through a forwarder that can't read it (C3 keying, TC-KEY1-4)

harness.mls: openmls 0.8.1 (real group: alice founder + bob + carol via add/welcome; remove = epoch advance)
harness.sframe_key: HKDF-SHA256(MLS exporter_secret epoch_proof, info = "croft/sframe/v1" || epoch_be || leaf_be) -> 32 B per (epoch, leaf)
harness.aead: ChaCha20-Poly1305; nonce = leaf||counter; clear header (epoch, leaf, counter) bound as AAD
harness.replay_window: sliding per-sender counter window (width 16); loss + intra-window reorder tolerant, no contiguity requirement
harness.note: pure crypto, no transport — the keying question is independent of the wire (E10 covers the wire/CC).

TC-KEY1.detail: alice and carol get distinct per-sender keys from the same exporter secret + leaf index; a non-member (no group) cannot call epoch_proof() -> cannot derive any key (media analog of the faithful-path UnauthorizedAuthor)

TC-KEY2.detail: 100 frames, 10 dropped, adjacent-pair reordered: 90/90 surviving frames decrypt OUT OF ORDER; a full replay of the stream is rejected 90/90 by the window

TC-KEY3.detail: removing carol advances the MLS epoch 1->2 and rotates the exporter secret; removed carol is stuck at epoch 1 (no S2); her later frame is rejected (stale epoch + non-member = media MD-G5); she cannot forge an epoch-2 frame; bob's cached pre-revocation frame still decrypts (history not clawed back); a current member (alice) still sends+decrypts at epoch 2

TC-KEY4.detail: the blind SFU holds no exporter secret: it routes/selects all 92 frames from the clear (epoch, leaf, counter) headers yet recovers 0 plaintext bytes; ciphertext never contains plaintext (extends E3.4/AR-4 blindness to media)

verdict: green-real: SFrame keyed off a REAL MLS group's exporter secret realizes the DAVE-shape blind-SFU media E2EE. Per-sender keys, loss-tolerant out-of-order decrypt with replay rejection, epoch-rotation revocation (media MD-G5 + history retention), and a content-blind forwarder all hold against openmls 0.8.1. C3 keying is de-risked.
open_edges: Synthetic media payloads (string frames), not real Opus/RTP packetization — keying is proven; codec integration is separate.
SFrame header is modeled (epoch, leaf, counter); a wire-format SFrame header (RFC 9605) is a later detail.
Run locally (pure crypto); a transport-carried version over iroh datagrams = the E10 rig + this keying, a small follow-on.

MLS key-distribution itself is real here (openmls add/welcome/remove); the broader 'key registry over the wire' honesty boundary (Workstream C) is about the faithful messaging crate, not this media path.
